metric_value: Microsoft delivers on its launch marketing promises for Windows XP
notes: Kastner's hands-on endorsement; 'this time Microsoft delivers'

metric_value: New high level of support for digital audio, digital video playing, digital moviemaking, and digital photography
notes: Kastner's direct assessment; specific to media capabilities

metric_value: XP makes connecting to wireless LANs 'a snap' — zero-configuration wireless
notes: Kastner's direct assessment of wireless ease of use

metric_value: WEP passwords must be converted into hexadecimal — practical user friction point noted
notes: Kastner's practical note; WEP hex conversion was user experience issue

metric_value: WEP proven cryptographically broken; replaced by WPA (Wi-Fi Protected Access) standard in 2003
notes: The WEP issue Kastner noted was minor UX; actual outcome was full security deprecation

metric_value: Almost all previous Windows users are going to enjoy living with Windows XP
notes: Kastner's core adoption prediction; 'almost all' is a strong claim

metric_value: XP achieved 12-14% adoption in first year; less than 10% of Microsoft's installed base upgraded by end of 2002 per Gartner
notes: Slower initial adoption than Kastner's 'almost all' suggests; corporate resistance due to Win2000 stability

metric_value: XP became most-used Windows version; maintained 35%+ share a decade after launch; still 1.44%+ of devices in 2022
notes: Long-term: Kastner's positive assessment was fully validated by XP's extraordinary longevity

metric_name: Microsoft digital content strategy with XP
metric_value: Microsoft positioned XP as Personal Information Technology and digital home networking platform
notes: Keywords: Personal Information Technology, Home Networking, Digital Content — Aberdeen's category framing

metric_name: Corporate XP adoption by end of 2002
metric_value: 75% of corporate PCs still running Windows 95, 98, or NT Workstation by end of 2002 (Gartner)
notes: Enterprise lagged behind consumer adoption; XP's consumer features not main enterprise driver

metric_value: For all of the launch marketing hype, this time Microsoft delivers
notes: Kastner's implicit reference to prior Windows launch overpromising (e.g., Windows ME)
